content.problemStatement:
You are managing a garden with an NNN X NNN grid of flower pots. Each row and column of the grid is arranged in ascending order of flower heights. Your task is to find the kkk-th smallest flower height in the entire garden when all the heights are arranged in sorted order.

Note that the kkk-th smallest flower height refers to its position in the sorted order, not its uniqueness. Write a solution without taking any extra space.


Input Format

The first line contains two space separated integers NNN and kkk, representing the size of the garden and the value of kkk.
The next NNN lines each contain NNN space-separated integers, where each integer represents the height of the flower at that position in the garden grid.


Output Format

A single integer representing the kkk-th smallest flower height in the garden.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and k. Then N lines follow, each containing N space‑separated integers denoting the garden matrix. Rows and columns of the matrix are sorted in non‑decreasing order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the k‑th smallest flower height in the given garden matrix.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All elements in sorted order are [1,5,9,10,11,12,13,13,15]; the 8th smallest is 13.
Only one element exists, so it is the 1st smallest.

solution.approach: Because every row and every column is sorted, for any value x we can count in O(N) how many elements are <= x by scanning from the top‑right corner and moving left/down. This monotonic count allows a binary search on the answer space (the range of values in the matrix). The smallest value for which count >= k is the k‑th smallest element. The algorithm runs in O(N log(maxVal‑minVal)) per test case and uses O(1) extra space besides the input matrix.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * log(maxElement - minElement)) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (in‑place counting), besides the input matrix
